headline: Negeri Sembilan Hill Land Development Doubles in 5 Years
context: JPBD data shows approved hill land development in Negeri Sembilan doubled from 1,200 hectares in 2019 to 2,400 hectares in 2024, concentrated around Seremban and Port Dickson.

cards.big: Hill land development in Negeri Sembilan doubled to 2,400 hectares in 5 years.
cards.sub: This coincides with a 180% increase in minor landslides recorded by JKR in the same districts.

cards.big: Federal guidelines restrict development above 300m elevation and 25-degree slopes.
cards.sub: Negeri Sembilan approved 14 projects exceeding both thresholds under state-level exemptions.

cards.big: Premium hilltop properties sell for 3-4x equivalent flatland prices.
cards.sub: Developer margins on hill projects exceed 40% — double the industry average.

cards.big: Highland Towers collapsed in 1993 killing 48 people.
cards.sub: 32 years later, the same structural factors — hillside clearing, altered drainage, weak enforcement — persist.

cards.big: Highland Towers killed 48 people. We said never again. The same practices continue.
Hill development guidelines must become enforceable law, not advisory standards that states can override. The lives at risk cannot wait for another tragedy to prove the point.
